gaps.proposed_title: Two-agent live coordination proof: worktrees, overlap, scoped message, context injection, handoff
gaps.why: The s7ae epic's headline acceptance criterion is 'A live proof demonstrates at least two agents on one repo with separate worktrees, visible overlap/resource awareness, a scoped coordination message, context injection, and a handoff packet.' The confirmed child set {s7ae.1, s7ae.2, s7ae.3, ahqd, bby.9, d1y, pj8} contains no bead that owns this capstone: s7ae.1 is the envelope, s7ae.3 is the message/advisory mechanism, ahqd is a single-agent MCP-write observation, bby.9 is the renderer. The epic cannot be closed against its own AC without this proof, and it is exactly the kind of end-to-end demo the priority frame ranks as a proof campaign (tier 1).
gaps.design: Reuse existing worktree tooling and the shipped surfaces. (1) Create two git worktrees of polylogue on separate branches; launch one Claude and one Codex full-profile agent, one per worktree. (2) Each agent runs `polylogue agents status --json` (or the MCP agent_coordination tool) and observes the sibling as a same-repo-agent overlap plus any live resource episodes (build/test/daemon) as non-blocking awareness. (3) Agent A posts a session-tree/repo-scoped coordination message (s7ae.3 / blackboard); agent B receives it via the SessionStart preamble delivery leg (37t.11 scheduler + d1y hooks). (4) Show the context-injection ledger row proving the coordination ContextSource entered B's context. (5) Agent A writes a HANDOFF packet under .agent/conductor-devloop/ and B consumes it via `polylogue agents handoff`. Capture all JSON envelopes + ledger rows as the durable proof artifact.
gaps.acceptance: A recorded run shows: two distinct agent sessions on one repo in separate worktrees; each agent's coordination envelope lists the other as same-repo overlap with blocking=false; at least one build/test/daemon resource episode surfaced as awareness; one scoped coordination message posted by A and delivered to B with provenance; one context-injection ledger row attributing the coordination source with its trust class; and a handoff packet authored by A and read back by B. Artifact stored under .local/ or a proof bead note; no step requires simulating rows outside a live agent session.

gaps.proposed_title: Judgment queue: operator bulk review/accept/reject of candidate assertions
gaps.why: The epic title names four stages (claims -> JUDGMENT -> preamble -> reboot) but no child owns judgment. It survives only as a phrase inside 37t.1's design ('the gap is operator-ergonomic review flow (bulk judge candidate batches)') and is assumed to already exist by 37t.10 ('the existing judgment queue lists setup candidates alongside memory candidates'). Verified today: list_assertion_claims exposes statuses='active,candidate' (server_tools.py:741) and per-item accept/reject MCP tools exist, but there is no batch review surface — so the pipeline has a stage with no execution-grade bead.
gaps.design: Build the operator-facing review surface over the existing candidate substrate: (1) a queue read that lists candidate-status assertions grouped by kind/repo/session with evidence ref preview (reuse list_assertion_claims candidate path); (2) bulk verbs — accept/reject/defer over a selected batch, each writing the judgment transition and preserving the origin ref; (3) a CLI entry ('polylogue judge' or under an existing group) + optional webui panel; (4) only ACCEPTED claims become preamble-eligible (ties to 37t.1's ACTIVE-only ASSERTION_CLAIM_KINDS read). This is the gate that makes background/declared candidate generation (37t.2, 37t.9 'dreaming') safe.
gaps.acceptance: Given N candidate assertions across mixed kinds, the queue lists them with evidence refs; a bulk accept promotes them to active and they become visible to the preamble compiler; a bulk reject removes them from candidacy; every transition is queryable with its origin ref; none of these operations mutate sessions.content_hash.

gaps.proposed_title: Content-variant demo asset: annotated session translated with transcript + assertion variants and clickable alignment
gaps.why: Epic 4smp AC explicitly requires 'A demo or fixture shows a heavily annotated session translated with transcript variants plus variants of selected assertion annotations, with clickable alignment back to original source and original assertions.' No child commits this as a deliverable: d4zk AC only covers 'demo fixtures cover translate heavily annotated session' and 'a coordination message containing clickable refs' — it does not commit the clickable-alignment-back-to-source-and-assertion demo asset. Compare ap7, which explicitly commits 'a before/after recording ... committed as the demo asset (3tl.5 machinery).' The variant epic's headline demo AC is currently ownerless. Alternative to a new child: strengthen d4zk acceptance to name this exact asset.
gaps.design: Reuse the deterministic demo archive (polylogue demo seed) + the 3tl.5 recording/asset machinery. Seed a session that carries several assertion annotations, produce a translation ContentVariant of the transcript plus variants of selected assertion bodies, render through the dual/interleaved variant render profile (rlsb), and capture a committed asset (web recording or rendered HTML/markdown fixture) demonstrating clickable alignment edges from variant nodes back to (a) original source blocks and (b) original assertions. Wire under the existing demo/proof lane rather than a bespoke script.
gaps.acceptance: A committed demo asset (fixture + optional recording) shows: transcript variant with per-node alignment back to source blocks; at least one assertion-annotation variant aligned back to its original assertion:<id>; a lossy summary node honestly labeled; source vs variant visually distinguished. Reproducible via a single `devtools workspace ...`/`polylogue demo ...` command and referenced from the epic. Serves the 3tl external-legibility goal.

gaps.proposed_title: Single-compiler parity gate: CLI/MCP/API/daemon route identical query strings through one grammar
gaps.why: The epic thesis is 'one grammar owns query semantics ... not a second parser' (fnm epic + fnm.4 description), and MCP already exposes explain_query_expression as the shared compiler entry, but no child asserts that a given query string produces the same typed AST / same lowered SessionQuerySpec across CLI find, MCP search/explain, the Python API, and the daemon/web query route. fnm.4 gates completion coverage and fnm.11 gates the docs matrix, but neither gates surface-execution parity — a regression could reintroduce per-surface parsing divergence silently. Operator memory repeatedly flags cross-surface parity as a live concern.
gaps.design: A parametrized parity test feeds a corpus of representative query strings (bare find, unit-where, pipeline with group by|count, with-projection, SEQ) through each surface's public entry and asserts the resulting QueryExpression/SessionQuerySpec (or to_payload) is byte-identical. Guardrail assertion: grep/import-lint that no surface module constructs a Lark parser or hand-rolls query parsing except polylogue/archive/query/expression.py. Wire into render all --check or devtools verify layering.
gaps.acceptance: The parity test covers >=1 query per grammar entry shape and asserts identical AST/spec across CLI/MCP/API/daemon; a second parser or divergent parse in any surface module fails the gate; explain_query_expression output matches the CLI-compiled spec for the corpus.

gaps.proposed_title: Chunk full insight rebuild into bounded per-batch transactions
gaps.why: 1xc's own design names this as a tier-1 confirmed-live item: 'full insight rebuild runs as ONE transaction -> 6GB WAL + minutes-long write lock on the live archive'. No child bead tracks it. 6h7's close note ('bounded 100-session ticks') fixed the daemon catch-up selector, not the full ops-reset rebuild transaction; 20d.15 owns bulk throughput and b5l owns downtime, neither owns the single-transaction/WAL blowup.
gaps.design: Chunk the storage/insights rebuild path so materialization commits in bounded per-batch transactions (e.g. N sessions per commit) with durable progress rows, so the WAL never grows to multi-GB and the write lock is released between batches. Reconcile with 6h7's existing 100-session daemon tick so the ops-reset rebuild and daemon catch-up share one bounded batching primitive rather than two.
gaps.acceptance: A full insight rebuild on a live-archive copy keeps WAL under a stated cap (e.g. the 160MiB journal_size_limit, not 6GB) and never holds the write lock for more than one batch interval; progress rows advance monotonically and a mid-rebuild kill resumes without redoing committed batches.

gaps.proposed_title: Audit global PKs derived from non-unique local coordinates (run_ref collision siblings)
gaps.why: 1xc's design: 'the run_ref global-PK collision class was fixed (#2464) -- audit for siblings (any global PK derived from non-unique local coordinates)'. The instance bead (polylogue-85z0 'Run projection subagent run collides with child main run_ref') is CLOSED, but the sibling-audit half of the tier-1 item has no bead.
gaps.design: Enumerate every table whose primary/unique key is composed from local coordinates that are only unique within a parent scope (position indexes, native offsets, per-session counters) and prove global uniqueness or add the missing parent qualifier. Prime suspects flagged in iec: message_id (session_id + ':' + native/position), block_id compounds, run_ref/observed-event keys. Add a scale-tier fixture that forces two parents with colliding local coordinates for each suspect key.
gaps.acceptance: A table-by-table matrix of global-PK keys with a uniqueness verdict (safe / needs-qualifier); each 'needs-qualifier' either fixed with a schema slice or filed as its own bead; a scale-tier regression test reproduces the run_ref-class collision shape and would fail on a regression.

gaps.proposed_title: Scale-regression lane: seeded large-archive tier that catches each shipped scale-bug class
gaps.why: 1xc's AC literally requires it: 'a scale-regression lane exists (seeded large-archive tier or live-copy probe) that would have caught each shipped bug class, wired into the optional lanes.' No bead owns building this lane, so the epic can never reach its stated terminal state.
gaps.design: Extend the synthetic corpus generator to emit a scale-tier fixture (16K+ sessions, 5M+ messages, duplicate native ids, giant single artifacts approximating the 384MB Codex raw row) and register it as an optional validation lane (devtools lab lanes). Each closed 1xc child adds a regression assertion against this fixture (WAL cap for the insight rebuild, planner/no-scan for ma2, bounded stop for 0ns/6wnh, PK-collision for the run_ref siblings).
gaps.acceptance: An optional lane runs the scale fixture and asserts each fixed scale-bug class stays fixed; the lane is listed in devtools lab lanes --list and referenced from each 1xc child's acceptance; running it on a pre-fix commit reproduces at least one of the shipped bug classes.

gaps.proposed_title: Config bead: classify every setting by governance role (startup/policy/user-pref/provider-cost/presentation/ops)
gaps.why: Operator doctrine requires the config surface to classify settings by role, but jnj.9's scope statement only covers 'ownership per layer, effective-value inspection, recovery from invalid config' — it never enumerates the settings taxonomy. Without the classification, 'one coherent config surface' cannot decide which layer owns which knob. This is additive design that jnj.9 currently lacks (design=null); fold into jnj.9 rather than a separate bead if preferred.
gaps.design: Enumerate every runtime setting and tag it with a governance role: startup (bind host/port, archive root), policy (retention, GC thresholds, embedding cost caps), user-pref (color, default view, format), provider-cost (voyage key, model, cost cap), presentation (themes, layout profiles), ops (daemon telemetry, cursor tuning). The role determines the authoritative owning layer (NixOS/HM vs user polylogue.toml vs CLI flag vs env). `polylogue config` effective-value inspection reports value + winning layer + role for each key. Recovery: invalid config in one role must not brick unrelated roles.
gaps.acceptance: Every setting appears in a role-tagged registry; `polylogue config` (or config show --effective) prints role + owning layer + effective value per key; a doc table maps role->authoritative layer; an invalid value in one role is reported without preventing startup of surfaces that do not depend on it.

gaps.proposed_title: Fix per-model provider-usage partitioning double-count (#2472 residual)
gaps.why: 5hf's own text names 'lineage-tokens (double-count)' as a companion, and usage.py:1406 documents that summing per-model rows double-counts session totals; operator memory records this as the residual #2472 partitioning bug still open after #2469. It is implied by 5hf's correctness scope, not invented.
gaps.design: Ensure per-model usage rows are exposed as a drill-down that never re-sums into the session total; the session total is computed once from disjoint token lanes. Add a regression test with a multi-model session asserting session_total != sum(per_model_totals) when lanes overlap. Ground the fix in storage/usage.py partitioning path (~1406, ~1528 caveat).
gaps.acceptance: A multi-model Hermes/Codex/Claude session reports one honest session total; per-model rows are drillable; the double-count regression test passes; the caveat at usage.py:1528 is only emitted when genuinely multi-model.

inconsistencies.evidence: s7ae.1 design says the envelope's inputs are archive session evidence: 'session topology, run/session events, tool/action blocks, recent activity ... blackboard/coordination messages, proof/outcome summaries, and context-flow refs.' The in-progress implementation (polylogue/coordination/envelope.py) derives peers, resource episodes, and overlap from the live OS process table (`ps -eo ...`, lines 36/307-354) and /proc, plus git and .beads/bd; it touches the archive only to read PRAGMA user_version on the tier DBs (_archive_payload lines 427-454). It reads NO session topology, session/run events, tool/action blocks, blackboard messages, or context-flow refs.
inconsistencies.fix: Reconcile the bead with the build: either (a) amend s7ae.1's design/acceptance to state that live agent/resource presence is derived from the process table + git + Beads (a legitimate 'who else is active right now' evidence source that the archive cannot give in real time), and move archive-session-derived fields (session topology, tool/action blocks, blackboard messages, context-flow refs) to explicit 'remaining' or to s7ae.3/bby.9; or (b) record that the archive-evidence legs are still unimplemented so the AC ('reusable envelope over existing archive evidence', 'blackboard/coordination messages' as inputs) is not prematurely marked satisfied. The process-table approach is defensible but is a different evidence model than the bead currently describes.

inconsistencies.evidence: 6mv is a type=decision whose own text says 'Status: adopt as working doctrine' and names concrete residual work: 'the Sinex-side emitter is future work gated on the substrate write-leg and Sinex production restore.' inconsistencies.evidence: 37t.11 has exactly two dependency edges: parent-child to 37t, and a 'relates-to' to s7ae. dep_priority.detail: 6kh design: 'Implemented as an implicit macro layer: the resolved prefs compile to a predicate group prepended to bare queries (the fnm.12 expansion machinery — visible in explain as implicit-scope)' (domain file line 368). 6kh currently depends only on y4c (settings registry); it also structurally depends on fnm.12's macro-expansion/provenance machinery to render implicit-scope in explain.
dep_priority.proposed: Add dependency: 6kh depends_on fnm.12 (or at minimum on the shared expansion/provenance layer). Otherwise 6kh must build its own expansion path, contradicting its stated reuse of fnm.12.

dep_priority.detail: fnm.7 design: 'This is the query form of outcome-conditioned analytics — coordinate with the analytics program bead so they share the relation' (domain file line 263). The shared relation is the actions/child aggregation surface that fnm.1 aggregates also lower onto, but no dep or explicit cross-link to fnm.1 exists.
dep_priority.proposed: Add a relates-to link fnm.7 <-> fnm.1 (or a soft dep) so the correlated-aggregate-subquery relation is built once and shared, not forked between child-count predicates and the aggregate pipeline.

dep_priority.detail: 38x's seed findings include 'Codex FORK vs RESUME conflation' and 'Codex token lane normalizer divergence' — exactly the residual that ivsc ('Classify Codex state_5 token drift outside lineage replay') was split out to own (from closed 4ts.2). 38x currently has relates-to edges to 4ts and 83u (confirmed via bd show) but NOT to ivsc, and ivsc has zero dependencies.
dep_priority.proposed: Add a relates-to edge 38x -> ivsc so the Codex-token-drift seed finding is explicitly linked to its owning executable bead. Separately, 38x's seed 'child usage rollups counting inherited prefix' is already FIXED by the CLOSED 4ts.2 and should be recorded as fixed (cite 4ts.2 close reason) in 38x's reconciliation table rather than re-triaged.

dep_priority.detail: gjg's FORENSICS part diffs a pre-compaction snapshot against the post-compact continuation's early context using 'the exact assembled context'. 4ts.5 delivers session_events boundary_start/end_position + boundary_message_id + get_effective_context(session, at_position) — the effective-context substrate gjg's loss measure needs to know what the model actually saw vs the full composed prefix. They overlap on compaction context derivation.
dep_priority.proposed: Add a dependency gjg (forensics) -> 4ts.5 (compaction boundary columns / effective-context), or explicitly note in both beads that 4ts.5 provides the effective-context primitive gjg's loss forensics consumes, to avoid two divergent compaction-boundary models.

dep_priority.detail: Epic AC terminal state includes 'composed correctly (read paths serve full logical transcripts across the branch-point matrix)'. Open children fix specific composition bugs (4ts.3 subagent-acompact, 4ts.4 read-txn atomicity) but no bead asserts a full composition-correctness matrix across all edge types (fork / resume / subagent / acompact / inline-compact / spawned-fresh). The closed 4ts.1 gate samples only prefix-sharing children through the composed read path.
dep_priority.proposed: Either extend the 4ts.1 lineage-validation gate to assert composed-read correctness across every branch-point edge type (not just prefix-sharing sampling), or add a small child that runs a branch-point-matrix composition test on the live archive; make it the citable proof of the 'composed correctly across the branch-point matrix' criterion before the epic closes.

dep_priority.detail: No child bead covers any of the epic's three AC obligations (tier-1 insight-rebuild chunking, run_ref sibling audit, scale-regression lane). The epic is P1 but currently un-actionable as an epic because it has no decomposition.
dep_priority.proposed: Create the three gap beads above and reparent ma2/0ns/6wnh; only then can 'every child closed' mean anything.

dep_priority.detail: b5l (P1, spine, 'Blue-green index rebuilds') is blocked by 20d.15 (P2, bulk ingest throughput) per its dependency; b5l's own design says the 20d.15 dependency is what makes the double-write window trivial. A P1 spine bead is gated on a P2.
dep_priority.proposed: Either elevate 20d.15 to P1 (it is on the critical path of a P1 spine deliverable and owns the 20-40min downtime measurement), or weaken the b5l->20d.15 edge to 'related' since b5l's design already offers a slower-rebuild fallback (delta-replay) that does not require the 5-min target.

dep_priority.detail: hiu (P2, 'Collapse storage twins') is blocked by pf1 (P3, sync/async divergence diff). hiu's design step (0) makes pf1 a hard prereq ('reconcile the 10 documented divergences INTO the sync store ... BEFORE any wiring moves'). A P2 with a decided operator direction is gated on a P3.
dep_priority.proposed: Elevate pf1 to P2 to match the bead it blocks, or fold pf1's divergence-reconciliation into hiu step (0) as an explicit sub-task so the collapse is not stalled behind a lower-priority standalone.

dep_priority.detail: 3tl.10 (launch kit) design states 'the uplift result (cfk) decides how loud the memory claim gets (capability-phrased until then)' and the 3tl epic description names cfk 'the natural launch post'. Launch content is gated on cfk reporting, but there is no recorded dependency edge from 3tl.10 (or the epic) to polylogue-cfk.
dep_priority.proposed: Add a blocks/depends edge: polylogue-3tl.10 depends_on polylogue-cfk (the memory-claim volume in the announcement cannot be finalized until the uplift re-run reports). Keeps the launch-gate sequencing legible instead of living only in prose.

dep_priority.detail: The 212 epic has 6 children but acceptance_criteria=null — no portfolio done-state. The description carries a strong COMPOSITIONALITY RULE (every demo decomposes into product primitives; bespoke logic => file+build the primitive first) but nothing states when the portfolio is complete.
dep_priority.proposed: Add epic acceptance: every child demo (1) reproduces on the seeded corpus (seed 1843) AND has a live-archive operator variant, (2) decomposes into product primitives per the compositionality rule with any bespoke-logic gaps filed as their own primitive beads, (3) ships a recording via visual-tapes machinery (3tl.5) and a workflow-registry entry so it is a golden path not a doc, (4) every displayed number resolves to structural evidence via --explain.

dep_priority.detail: 20d.12 design explicitly states 'Sequence: lands with/after 20d.1 so CLI + webui + MCP all hit the same cache' — the cache is only worth building once the fast path routes callers through the daemon. But dependency_count=0 (only the parent-child edge exists).
dep_priority.proposed: Add dep: polylogue-20d.12 depends_on polylogue-20d.1.

dep_priority.detail: 20d.15 text sets up two relationships that are prose-only: '20d.6 owns the LIVE catch-up lane ... this bead owns the BULK lane' (clean split, but the shared WAL-autocheckpoint interplay is cross-cutting) and its throughput target '>=100 rows/s ... stated in the SLO catalog as a maintenance-tier budget (20d.14)'. dependency_count=0.
dep_priority.proposed: Add dep polylogue-20d.15 depends_on polylogue-20d.14 (its acceptance target is the budget 20d.14 must define). Record a coordinate/relates link to 20d.6 for the shared WAL/autocheckpoint measurement rather than a hard block.

dep_priority.detail: 20d.14 is the definitional hub: its design says 'The budgets become the acceptance criteria for the sibling beads: fast path, cache, push, import deferral all cite this tier instead of inventing their own targets.' Several siblings already cite it in prose (20d.15 -> maintenance-tier budget; 20d.12 -> SLO lane hit-rate; 20d.13 -> within 2s) but no dep edges record that 20d.1/20d.2/20d.12/20d.13 acceptance depends on 20d.14 defining the tier.
dep_priority.proposed: Treat 20d.14 as the budget anchor for the SLO story: add depends_on polylogue-20d.14 from 20d.1, 20d.2, 20d.12, and 20d.13 so their acceptance criteria reference the named budgets rather than re-inventing targets. Sequence 20d.14 (or at least its budget skeleton) ahead of the measured beads.

dep_priority.detail: 20d.8 description: 'Likely falls out of the action-unit outcome fields work (SQL-side pairing instead of Python row inspection). Re-measure after that lands.' 20d.10 in the same epic lowers the action matchers onto the actions view (SQL-side, keystone v16 columns). The 43s regen is an implied downstream of that SQL-side pairing work, but no dep is recorded and the upstream 'action-unit outcome fields work' bead isn't linked.
dep_priority.proposed: Add a soft dep / relates edge polylogue-20d.8 depends_on polylogue-20d.10 (or the specific action-unit-outcome bead if it lives in another epic) and mark 20d.8 as 're-measure after upstream lands' so it isn't worked before its prerequisite.

dep_priority.detail: dmp design: 'Same verb over MCP already exists once 27p lands (candidate writer) — keep payload shapes identical.' polylogue-27p (write-capable MCP / candidate writer) is a real bead (present in beads_index). dmp requires the candidate-writer substrate to land first, but no dependency edge exists.
dep_priority.proposed: Add dep: polylogue-dmp depends-on polylogue-27p (blocks). At minimum the shared candidate-writer/payload contract from 27p must exist before dmp can guarantee CLI/MCP note indistinguishability.

dep_priority.detail: jnj.3 (P3, 'Output dialect normalization ... remove aliases not buying value') and jnj.1 (P3, 'remove the aliases' after extending the spec algebra) both delete CLI aliases. t46 (P2 epic) explicitly owns alias deletion via named slices ('historical CLI aliases find_help/-n/--full/demo-shelf --bundle', 'status JSON compatibility aliases', 'config aliases'). Two epics assert ownership of the same 'delete parallel/alias surface' doctrine at different priorities with no sequencing.
dep_priority.proposed: Sequence t46 (P2, replacement-first alias/bridge deletion) ahead of jnj.1/jnj.3 (P3), and partition the alias inventory: t46 owns historical/compat/config aliases; jnj owns per-view-flag and output-dialect aliases that only exist once the ProjectionSpec/RenderSpec algebra is extended. Add a relates-to/depends-on edge between jnj.3 and t46 so the same alias is not deleted twice or left orphaned.

dep_priority.detail: The epic claims 'agents and users should be able to infer one rule for output format, destination, projection, and mutation safety' and 'Absorbs gh#2317 and gh#2309', but has no acceptance criteria defining that end-state, and no way to tell when the four 'one rule per concern' invariants are satisfied.
dep_priority.proposed: Add epic AC: (1) exactly one output-format mechanism, one destination mechanism, one projection algebra, and one destructive-mutation contract remain across CLI/MCP; (2) gh#2317 scope (root onboarding/facets — jnj.2, jnj.8) and gh#2309 scope (config — jnj.9) each have a covering child that is closed; (3) a CLI-surface-audit test asserts no verb carries a boolean-mode mux or a per-view flag that duplicates the spec algebra.

dep_priority.detail: The epic bby is P3, but its child bby.11 is P1 (spine, wave:2) and two P1 epics (4smp, s7ae) relate-to bby.11 for their web/mission-control/variant projections (per their design text: 4smp anchors 'bby.11 and 90y for web/in-page UX'; s7ae uses 'bby.11 only where the web architecture must carry the projection'). A P3 epic reads as deep backlog while it actually gates P1 cross-epic web work.
dep_priority.proposed: Lift epic bby to P2 to reflect that it carries P1-blocking architecture, OR reparent bby.11 out of bby into a foundational/spine epic and leave the remaining bby children at P3. The status quo hides a P1 spine decision inside a deep-backlog epic.

dep_priority.detail: bby.8 is P2 — higher than the epic (P3) and most siblings — for a perceived-performance web feature, but its premise (retrofit the inline-string SPA) may be obsoleted by the P1 bby.11 rewrite. Under the priority frame (proof/enablers/correctness > surface hygiene), a possibly-throwaway web-perf feature at P2 outranks its own foundation.
dep_priority.proposed: Demote bby.8 to P3 and gate it on the bby.8-vs-bby.11 scope decision, or fold it into bby.11 (its techniques are already listed as bby.11 foundation items).

dep_priority.detail: bby.9 (web mission control) and rlsb (variant-aware reader render) are P1 and render against the v2 web stack, but they only relate-to bby.11 — there is no depends/blocks edge asserting bby.11 provides their scaffold. If v2 is the target web surface, their web legs cannot land before the scaffold exists.
dep_priority.proposed: Either add blocks edges bby.11 -> (bby.9 web-render leg, rlsb web dual-lane leg), or document in bby.11 that these views may render on the current SPA (so the sequencing is intentional, not accidental). Operator already hedged with 'bby.11 only where the web architecture must carry the projection' — make that boundary explicit as an edge or a note rather than leaving it as an unlabeled relates-to.

dep_priority.detail: fs1.4 (P2, forensics report / flagship demo) has a blocks-dependency on fs1.1 (P3, Hermes state.db importer). fs1.1 also blocks fs1.5 and fs1.6. A P3 blocker gating the P2 deliverable is a priority inversion — the keystone importer + wiring-completion (keystone outcome extraction, real fixtures) is prerequisite to every downstream Hermes bead.
dep_priority.proposed: Raise fs1.1 to P2 to match the highest bead it blocks (fs1.4). Alternatively, if the Hermes program is genuinely gated (epic says priority rises when an artifact is scheduled), drop fs1.4 to P3 so the whole fs1 subtree is P3-consistent — but do not leave a P2 blocked by P3.

dep_priority.detail: o21 (declare-once across ALL extension points; wave:1) explicitly lists origins as one of its extension points ('a new origin touches detector, parser, enum, schema package, usage-coverage, completeness matrix') and sequences 'pilot on MCP tools, extract the pattern, then sweep the rest one per PR'. 2qx (OriginSpec) IS the origin sweep — the deep, strictness-tier-derived instantiation of o21's declare-once pattern for the origin extension point.
dep_priority.proposed: Add a dependency 2qx depends-on o21 (o21 extracts the declare-once pattern first) and tag 2qx wave:2 so it follows o21's wave:1. Keep them separate beads: 2qx carries origin-specific value (derived dispatch by strictness tier) beyond a mechanical pattern application.

dep_priority.detail: rii epic carries an OPERATOR GATE ('full program needs explicit green-light before a large build') and per focus-hint overlaps s7ae (coordination) and 37t (context loop): rii.1's agent work-event write-leg is adjacent to 37t's in-loop context read and s7ae's coordination writes. The boundary of which program owns the generic write-leg vs the context-read-back vs coordination is not pinned in rii's epic (design=null).
dep_priority.proposed: Add an epic design/boundary note on rii stating: rii owns the generic write-leg + intake seams (record_work_event->session_events->read-models); 37t owns the read-back/context-compilation loop; s7ae owns multi-agent coordination semantics. Orchestrator should reconcile against s7ae/37t records (not in this slice) before marking rii.1 ready given the operator gate.

dep_priority.detail: mhx.1 depends_on polylogue-37t.5 (type blocks), i.e. the general provider abstraction is recorded as BLOCKED BY its own local-lane demo. mhx.1's design says the reverse: 37t.5 collapses into mhx.1's acceptance. The dependency direction is backwards.
dep_priority.proposed: Reverse to 37t.5 depends_on mhx.1 (or drop the edge and reparent 37t.5 as mhx.1's demo child). The abstraction is the enabler; the local-lane demo is a downstream verification of it.

dep_priority.detail: Because the emb-targets/emb-eval alias edges do not resolve, mhx.4 and mhx.5 have NO effective blocking dependency on the session/assertion vector classes they consume (mhx.2), and mhx.6 has no effective dependency on the quality gate (mhx.3) it explicitly says it must sequence after ('efficiency without a quality gate is how retrieval silently degrades').
dep_priority.proposed: Add real edges: mhx.4 blocks-on mhx.2, mhx.5 blocks-on mhx.2, mhx.6 blocks-on mhx.3. Ready frontier is then correctly just mhx.2 and mhx.3.

dep_priority.detail: The mhx epic has a rich description but design=null and acceptance_criteria=null, and none of its children carry acceptance either, so the epic has no rollup done-state. The description enumerates five owned gaps (provider generality, embed-target policy, measured retrieval, lifecycle honesty, advanced uses) but there is no acceptance line tying child completion to epic closure.
dep_priority.proposed: Add epic acceptance: epic closes when mhx.1 (generality), mhx.2 (targets), mhx.3 (measured retrieval + default updated from evidence), and lifecycle-honesty pair (0k6 staleness + mhx.6 spend/drain) are closed; mhx.4/mhx.5 (recall/analytics) may spin to follow-ups if the eval (mhx.3) does not justify their cost.

dep_priority.detail: b0b (replace keyword heuristics) has dependency_count 0, but 9e5.3 states it is 'the go/no-go gate for the heuristic->structural sweep bead' and 9e5.9 states it 'produces the labeled fixture set that sweep needs for regression'. Both are prerequisites asserted in prose but unrecorded.
dep_priority.proposed: Add b0b depends_on 9e5.3 (structural-column coverage go/no-go) and b0b depends_on 9e5.9 (accuracy baseline + labeled fixtures). Also link b0b relates-to the claim-vs-evidence campaign (devtools/claim_vs_evidence.py exists) since b0b's tool_result_is_error/exit_code consumption is that campaign's analytics stratum.

dep_priority.detail: 9e5.16 design explicitly builds its capability matrix from 'facade methods (9e5.14 map)', so it cannot start before 9e5.14 produces that map. dependency_count on .16 is 0; .14 shows dependent_count 1 (edge asserted somewhere but not the .16 parent-independent dep).
dep_priority.proposed: Add 9e5.16 depends_on 9e5.14 (facade decomposition map).

dep_priority.detail: 9e5.15 (systematic dead-code sweep) design names 9e5.5 (dead-table kill list) as one of the already-beaded component signals it intersects/complements; its table-level output feeds the sweep's coverage picture.
dep_priority.proposed: Add 9e5.15 relates-to (or depends_on) 9e5.5 so the table-level and symbol-level dead-weight audits are sequenced, not run twice from scratch.

reorg.to: new epic 'Beads/devloop work-evidence rail' grouping 4c0, 7fj, lnd, lio, kph (and 6mv's emitter task)
reorg.rationale: 4c0 (session<->bead join), 7fj (beads-history ingestion, its substrate dep), lnd (beads-vs-assertions decision), lio (cross-repo devloop contract on beads), and kph (provenance-carrying PRs) form one coherent theme — joining external work-tracking/PR evidence with the archive — that is distinct from s7ae's live-agent coordination. They are currently all parent=null. An epic would make 7fj's blocking role and the decisions' scope explicit instead of a loose standalone cluster. Medium confidence: only propose if the orchestrator agrees the cluster is real; do not force-fit h6r (it belongs to the analytics/uplift tower, blocking h10) into it.

ap7 and 1lm are standalone parentless P2 read-algebra tasks, yet both explicitly extend the same substrate: 1lm 'Extend ProjectionSpec (jnj.1) ... 4p1's Projection axis, deepened'; ap7 'Layout profiles ... RenderSpec presets per 4p1.' fnm ('Query DSL: one grammar owns query semantics') and jnj ('Product surface algebra') are the read-algebra epics. Parenting ap7 (and 1lm) under the read-algebra epic makes the program that 4smp depends on visible as one deliverable rather than three floating tasks. Low confidence — they may be intentionally standalone; if so, at minimum link them under 4p1/fnm so the read-algebra frontier is auditable as a unit.

gjg is typed 'task' with parent=null but its design carries four epic-sized workstreams (PreCompact snapshot capture, corpus-wide compaction-loss forensics measure, opt-in re-grounding SessionStart lane armable as an ExperimentSpec, and the 37t handoff-triad documentation) and it is already dep-linked to s7ae via d1y and references the 37t epic. Promote to an epic (or at minimum reparent under s7ae) and decompose into children so each part becomes independently ready/verifiable rather than one unschedulable mega-task.

ma2 (FK index for web_content_constructs message cleanup) is a textbook 1xc case: correct on small fixtures, SCAN over ~89k rows on the live v24 rebuild. Its blockers 3wb and 6h7 are both now CLOSED, so it is ready-frontier and gives the empty epic a concrete, verifiable child.

0ns (bound embedding work within large sessions) is exactly 1xc's stated class: 'code correct on small/clean fixtures but wrong at real scale ... giant single artifacts'. Unbounded per-session embedding only bites on huge Codex/Claude sessions.

6wnh (bound thread_refresh for large Codex appends) is the same scale-bug class (3.02s of 3.04s graph_resolve on a 340.8MB Codex append). Its blocker 3wb is CLOSED, so it is ready. Reparenting ma2/0ns/6wnh converts the empty epic into a real 3-child decomposition.

Considered and REJECTED reparenting iec/hiu/b5l/exb under 1xc. 1xc's scope is confirmed-severe scale BUGS; iec is a proactive schema-cost audit, hiu/exb are refactors (twin collapse, layering), and b5l is an operational architecture change (P1 spine in its own right). They relate to scale but are not the 'wrong-at-scale bug' class 1xc owns; only the bound-cost/scan-bug beads (ma2/0ns/6wnh) belong under it.

3tl.4 produces the 'published finding URL' that is item (3) of the 3tl epic's OWN acceptance and is the named enforcement site for cpf's provenance stanza plus the citation target for 3tl.3/3tl.10/3tl.8. It is a wave:2/spine enabler on the epic's critical path sitting at P3 (deep-backlog tier) while its dependents also sit at P3 — the finding URL cannot exist until 3tl.4 lands, so it is under-prioritized relative to the frame (campaign enablers > surface hygiene).

reorg.to: keep standalone (do NOT move under polylogue-20d)
reorg.rationale: Answering the focus hint: k8k does not belong as a 20d child. Its fix is in the Sinnix repo (devshell scope wrapper), not Polylogue code; 20d children are Polylogue-code front-door perf work. Reparenting would misattribute ownership. Link it to 20d as external measurement context instead.

reorg.to: (standalone P2 correctness bug, or a security/mutation-safety bead)
reorg.rationale: jnj is a surface-hygiene program (correctly P3 in the priority frame). jnj.5 is a P2 area:security bug where `ops reset --session/--source` tombstones before any confirmation (reset.py:260-277). Nesting live correctness/security work under a hygiene epic makes the epic's priority ambiguous and risks the bug inheriting hygiene-tier deprioritization. Track it at its true priority independent of the polish program (keep a relates-to link to jnj for the shared mutation-contract theme).

reorg.to: new epic e.g. 'Capture extension: reliability, coverage, and in-page presence'
reorg.rationale: 3v1 (MV3 spool health/badge/completeness/gemini bridge/error telemetry) and 90y (in-page shadow-DOM overlay: archive state + assertion capture) are both P2, both area:ingest+area:web, and both scoped to the browser extension — a coherent cluster distinct from the daemon-served web workbench (bby is the daemon reader). They have no owning epic, so extension work is uncounted at the epic level. Group them (and future extension beads) under a capture-extension epic; do NOT reparent them under bby, since 3v1 is pure MV3 reliability with no daemon-reader surface. Caveat: verify no existing capture/extension epic already exists before creating a new one.

reorg.to: usage/cost-honesty epic parenting 5hf + lineage-token-double-count + cost-reconciliation companions
reorg.rationale: Answering the focus hint: 5hf must NOT go under fs1 — cost honesty is cross-provider correctness, not Hermes-specific. It names two companions (lineage-tokens double-count, cost-reconciliation probe) and the residual #2472 partitioning bug; those + 5hf form a coherent usage epic. If those companions already exist as beads elsewhere, parent them together; otherwise 5hf can stay standalone but must be specified. Keep it out of the Hermes bridge subtree either way.

reorg.to: epic with 4-5 children (capture/versioning, session-config join, skill-invocation report, one-section migration drill, config-doc surface)
reorg.rationale: Acceptance enumerates five independent multi-PR done-states and the design has four numbered legs; a single feature bead cannot honestly track it as one PR-shaped unit (see inconsistencies wrong-type).

0ns ('Bound archive embedding work within large sessions') is embedding daemon-drain work: _embed_archive_sessions_sync checks the stop-after-seconds bound only between sessions while embed_archive_session_sync can run a huge session past the daemon window. It is squarely embedding-lifecycle and overlaps mhx.6's DRAIN SCOPING item. Parent it under mhx and note the overlap with mhx.6 (windowed selection vs whole-session unit). Focus hint suggests 1xc (scale) as an alternative home; embedding-specific behavior argues for mhx, but if 1xc owns daemon responsiveness broadly, cross-link rather than fully move.

reorg.to: audit-lane (pure read-only artifacts) + audit-graduation execution epic
reorg.rationale: The epic is coherent as a catalog, but operator direction on 9e5.9 (make it a repeatable devtools lane) plus the acceptance criteria on .15 (merged deletion PRs, new lane) and .16 (verify gate + API fixes) show the natural arc is audit -> tooling/execution. Rather than let build tasks sit under a 'never mutates product code' epic, either (a) re-contract 9e5 to permit graduation and spin child execution beads for .9/.15/.16's build+merge halves, or (b) create a sibling execution epic that receives those halves. Low-churn preference: (a) plus splitting .15/.16 execution out; keep the read-only measurement halves in 9e5.
